content.thinking:
Now I need to update the ConfigMap to use `ray-head:6379` instead of `ray://ray-head:10001`. 

But there's a challenge: ConfigMap updates don't automatically propagate to mounted volumes immediately (there can be a delay of up to the kubelet sync period, typically 1 minute). And since the pod isn't running a long-lived process, just a single script execution when we exec into it, the volume will be updated when the kubelet syncs.

Also, since `ray start` was run on the pod, the local ray node is joined to the cluster. But if the pod restarts, the ray start won't be there anymore. 
The real issue is that the script needs a local ray node to connect to the cluster via `ray-head:6379`, and I've already started one on the pod. Once I update the ConfigMap with the new address, the script should work correctly. I'll update the ConfigMap and let the kubelet sync pick up the changes, then re-run the test to verify it works with the corrected Redis address.

done

content.thinking:
The pod command is:
```sh
echo "ray client ready"
sleep infinity
```

I need to recreate it with:
```sh
echo "ray client ready"
ray start --address=ray-head:6379
sleep infinity
```

This way, when the pod starts:
1. It prints "ray client ready"
2. It starts a local Ray node joined to the cluster at ray-head:6379
3. It keeps running with sleep infinity

Then when the test executes `python /opt/job.py`, the script with `ray.init(address="ray-head:6379")` will work because there's a local ray node running.
Now I'll delete the current pod and apply a new manifest with the updated command. Since the ConfigMap already has the correct content pointing to ray-head:6379, the new pod will pick it up automatically when it's created.

content.text: I need to recreate the pod with `ray start --address=ray-head:6379` in its startup command so the local ray node is ready for the script. I'll delete the pod and apply a new manifest.

input.command:
kubectl -n ray delete pod ray-client --grace-period=0 && cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/component: client
    app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
    bench.ray.cluster: ray
  name: ray-client
  namespace: ray
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  containers:
  - name: ray-client
    image: rayproject/ray:2.9.0
    command:
    - sh
    - -c
    - |
      echo "ray client ready"
      ray start --address=ray-head:6379
      sleep infinity
    volumeMounts:
    - mountPath: /opt/job.py
      name: job-script
      subPath: job.py
  volumes:
  - name: job-script
    configMap:
      name: ray-job-script
EOF
